title: Tick 26: ship RESUME md at repo root listing the top 5 files a fresh Claude session or new operator should read first on session resume
question: Does RESUME md at repo root list exactly 5 files in priority order each with a one-paragraph explanation of why it is on the list and end with a state-at-a-glance section that summarizes track status live infrastructure and the next-action recommendation?
success_criteria: RESUME md exists at the repo root not under docs or outcomes
RESUME md lists 5 files in priority order each with a one-paragraph rationale and a relative or absolute path so a reader can jump directly to each
the file ends with a state-at-a-glance section summarizing all 6 tracks done blocked and in_progress counts plus the live worker URL and the heartbeat cron job id
the file names what to do next when no operator guidance is given which currently is hold pattern because B5 is the only meaningful pending and it is explicitly held for human merge

capabilities: RESUME md at repo root with 5 ordered files
live worker URL and bearer token path documented
next-action recommendation explicit including the no-action-needed case
